title: Fix navbar transparency and link visibility issues

requirements.summary: Replace transparent navbar with solid opaque navy background that is always visible over all page backgrounds including dark hero sections
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar has a solid opaque navy (#0D1B4B) background on all pages, including the Home page with its dark hero section
Navbar text and links are always clearly readable (white text on navy background) with no transparency artifacts
Navbar renders above all page content with no z-index conflicts

file_operations.description: Remove all transparency-related classes (bg-transparent, bg-opacity-*, backdrop-blur-*) from the navbar container. Set className to include 'bg-[#0D1B4B]' (solid opaque navy) with 'z-50' to ensure the navbar always renders above page content. Ensure white text color for all nav links and buttons (text-white) for maximum contrast against the navy background. The navbar must have a solid background-color at all times with no transparency or blur effects.

requirements.summary: Ensure all desktop navigation links (Home, Dashboard, Admin Panel) and Login/Logout button are visible in a single horizontal row without clipping or overflow
acceptanceCriteria: On desktop, 'Home', 'Dashboard' (when authenticated), 'Admin Panel' (when admin), and the Login/Logout button are all visible in a single horizontal row
No nav link or button is clipped, wrapped, or hidden at standard desktop viewport widths
The 'Admin Panel' link appears only for authenticated admin users and is hidden for non-admin users
'Dashboard' link appears only when the user is authenticated

file_operations.description: Rewrite the desktop navigation layout to use a flex container with 'flex flex-nowrap items-center gap-6' for the nav links section. Ensure the three conditional nav links render in order: 'Home' (always visible, Link to='/'), 'Dashboard' (visible only when isAuthenticated, Link to='/dashboard'), and 'Admin Panel' (visible only when isAuthenticated AND isAdmin === true, Link to='/admin'). Position the Login/Logout button on the far right using 'ml-auto'. Verify sufficient horizontal spacing so all rendered items fit in a single row at md breakpoint and above without wrapping or clipping.

requirements.summary: Fix mobile hamburger menu to display all conditional links with solid opaque navy background and ensure hamburger icon is always visible
acceptanceCriteria: Mobile hamburger menu dropdown has a solid opaque navy background, not transparent
All conditional links are visible and readable in the mobile menu
Hamburger icon is visible on all page backgrounds
Mobile menu opens and closes reliably

file_operations.description: Rewrite the mobile menu dropdown container to have 'bg-[#0D1B4B]' (solid opaque navy) background instead of any transparent or semi-transparent background. Include the same three conditional links as desktop: 'Home' (always visible), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only), plus the Login/Logout action. Ensure the hamburger icon button uses white color (text-white) and has sufficient contrast against all backgrounds. Verify the mobile menu state toggle works correctly and the dropdown opens/closes reliably.

requirements.summary: Call useIsCallerAdmin query at component top level and conditionally render Admin Panel link only when authenticated and isAdmin === true with no loading flicker
acceptanceCriteria: Admin Panel link appears for the admin user in both desktop and mobile nav after login
Admin Panel link does not appear for non-admin authenticated users
Admin Panel link does not flicker or appear briefly for non-admin users during loading
No console.log debug statements remain in the component

file_operations.description: Call useIsCallerAdmin() at the top level of the Navbar component (import from '../hooks/useQueries'). Derive isAdmin by checking data === true. Conditionally render the 'Admin Panel' link in both desktop nav and mobile menu only when isAuthenticated AND isAdmin === true AND the query is not in a loading or fetching state. Remove any console.log or debug statements from prior iterations. Ensure the three nav links are exactly: 'Home' (always visible), 'Dashboard' (authenticated only), 'Admin Panel' (authenticated + isAdmin only).
